visual_features.overall_style: Playful, handcrafted, and slightly retro blog style with a fantasy/astrology mood. Uses a watercolor illustration header, rounded containers, pastel colors (mint, lavender), and bright accent text colors (green, purple, red). Overall feel is informal and friendly rather than modern-minimalist.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is created by: (1) a large illustrated header with the site name in a distinctive handwritten display font, (2) a wide, prominent navigation bar as a pill-shaped container, (3) a secondary pill-shaped breadcrumb bar, and (4) a large article title in purple, followed by metadata and body text in dark text. Color contrast (purple headings, green links) and size differences guide attention from the logo to navigation, then to the article title and content.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate to generous whitespace. Each major block (navigation, breadcrumb, article content) is placed inside a large rounded rectangle with inner padding and vertical spacing between blocks. Background lavender space around containers provides visual breathing room, though text inside cards is relatively compact and uses traditional paragraph spacing.
unique_elements: Watercolor sky/town illustration in the header, spanning full width
Playful handwritten logo with multi-colored word emphasis ('Vida' in purple, subtitle in red)
Stacked, oversized rounded-rectangle bars used for navigation and breadcrumbs with subtle outline or pseudo-shadow
Large hamburger and magnifying-glass icons floating over the header illustration
Language-switch flags (Portuguese and Spanish) aligned to the right in the header

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Single centered column with a loose underlying grid approximating a 12-column layout, constrained to a fixed max-width content area within full-width background sections.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Centered, stacked full-width sections inside a fixed-width content column. Classic blog layout with header illustration at the top, followed by navigation bars and then a content card.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top header section with illustration and logo; left-aligned floating icons (menu, search) and right-aligned language flags. Beneath the header, a large pill-shaped primary navigation bar. Below that, a slightly smaller pill-shaped breadcrumb bar. Main article content appears in a large rounded white card with title, metadata, and body text flowing vertically.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall content column is center-aligned on the page. Within containers, text is mostly left-aligned; navigation items are horizontally centered within the nav bar. Logo is right-of-center in the header illustration, while utility icons (menu/search) sit on the left and flags on the right, creating a balanced but informal alignment across the top.
responsive_hints: Presence of a prominent hamburger icon suggests a collapsible or off-canvas navigation for smaller screens.
Large, full-width pill containers likely collapse to full-width blocks on mobile with centered text.
Single-column layout is naturally mobile-friendly; little evidence of complex multi-column grids.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow with stacked full-width bars: an illustrated header at top, followed by a full-width navigation pill, then a full-width breadcrumb pill, then a large article card. Inside the article card, content is arranged in a classic vertical reading order: title at top, metadata row underneath, then paragraphs of text.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The illustrated header occupies roughly the top 25–30% of the visible viewport in this screenshot. The navigation and breadcrumb bars each occupy around 8–10% in height, with generous vertical margins between them. The main article card occupies the remaining visible space. Each card has substantial inner padding (approx. 16–24px) and rounded corners, with ample background space (lavender) visible between stacked elements.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderate density. There is clear separation between macro elements (cards/bars) with sizeable gaps, but within the article card, text is relatively standard-density with normal paragraph spacing and no excessive padding. Navigation bar text is well spaced and not crowded.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye starts at the colorful watercolor header and logo (top center-right), then moves left to the large hamburger/search icons, then down to the strong horizontal navigation pill (central). From there, the eye follows vertically to the breadcrumb bar, then to the prominent purple article title. The content then reads downward in a strong vertical flow through the text.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons are implied by navigation and likely other CTAs: wide rounded rectangles with corner radius around 16–24px, giving a pill-like appearance. Backgrounds use solid pastel mint (#C3E9DE) or possibly white for inactive states with a darker outline or subtle outer shadow. Text is medium-weight sans or serif in a saturated color (deep blue or green). Buttons are flat (no gradient) with a slight border or shadow to separate from the lavender background.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Typically implemented as elements styled as buttons inside a flex or inline-block container. Markup pattern: Label or navigation anchors where the parent
  • or
    provides the pill background and padding.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards are large rounded rectangles with substantial corner radius (~20–24px), white or very light colored backgrounds, and a thin dark border or soft drop shadow to lift them off the lavender page background. Inner padding is generous (16–24px on all sides). The article card includes a centered title at the top, followed by left-aligned text content.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit form visible in this screenshot, but style is implied by the general aesthetic: likely rounded input fields with soft borders matching the mint/lavender palette, white input background, and dark text. Labels probably sit above inputs, with simple, non-material styling and normal line-height.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected pattern: stacked labels with full-width inputs, using <label> directly above <input> or <textarea>. Groups of fields wrapped in <div class="form-group"> with vertical spacing; submit button styled similarly to navigation buttons (pill-shaped).

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal bar inside a large pill-shaped container spanning almost the full width of the content column. Menu items (Chinese Astrology, Tarot, Yearly Predictions, etc.) are spaced evenly across the bar, using a strong text color (deep blue) on a pastel mint background. The bar has rounded corners with a thin dark border or subtle shadow. The breadcrumb navigation below is another pill container, slimmer in height, with dark green link text centered within.

wrapper or a
with inline links.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Within the snippet we see mainly paragraphs, but typical lists in this theme would likely be text-focused, with standard bullet or numbered styles, left-aligned within the card. Density is moderate, with normal line-height and a little extra margin between list items. No visible separators; whitespace is the main separator.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Standard HTML <ul>/<ol> with <li> items, inheriting typography from the card. For blog lists (e.g., list of articles) likely implemented as vertical stacks of link titles inside a container, each in its own <div> or <li> with margin-bottom spacing.

heading.visual_style: Logo uses a playful, handwritten display font with rounded strokes and irregular character shapes; article headings appear to use a traditional serif (similar to Times/Georgia) in a bold or semi-bold weight and colored purple.
heading.recommended: For logo/primary display headings: 'Kalam' or 'Amatic SC' for a handwritten vibe; for article titles: 'Lora' for an elegant, slightly calligraphic serif that matches the mystical/astrology mood.

body.visual_style: Readable classic serif body text, fairly neutral, with moderate contrast and traditional proportions, suited for long-form reading.
body.recommended: Libre Baskerville or Cormorant Garamond as a refined yet readable body serif that fits an editorial astrology blog.

spacing_system.component_padding: Cards and pill containers typically use 16–24px padding on all sides, with text not touching edges and headings given a bit more top/bottom space.
spacing_system.section_margin: Sections (nav bar, breadcrumb, article card) are separated vertically by approx. 16–24px of margin, creating clearly distinct bands of content against the lavender background.

interaction_hints: Navigation links and inline text links likely change color (e.g., darker shade or underline) on hover, given traditional web styling.
Pill-shaped navigation bars may apply a slightly darker pastel background or stronger shadow on hover/active states to convey clickability.
Hamburger and search icons in the header are large tap targets; clicking may open an off-canvas menu or overlay search panel with a simple fade/slide transition.
Subtle transitions (150–250ms ease-in-out) on hover for background-color and box-shadow would match the soft, dreamy aesthetic without feeling too modern or harsh.
